req.title: Beneficiary management (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall add a beneficiary which enters a 24-hour cooling-off period before it can receive transfers. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-086 covers three orthogonal concerns: (1) happy-path beneficiary creation entering pending/cooling-off state, (2) idempotency via Idempotency-Key, (3) duplicate account rejection with 409. A fourth test covers the boundary between pending and active states at the 24-hour mark (state transition). Together these form a minimal yet complete suite using EP, NEG, BVA, STC, and DT heuristics. Each test is self-contained via fixture teardown and uses a unique idempotency key seeded from the test name to remain deterministic.

tests.name: test_add_beneficiary_returns_pending_state
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid JWT available via <auth_fixture>)
Target account number does not already exist for this customer
actions: Generate unique idempotency key and unique account number
POST /beneficiaries with valid payload and Idempotency-Key header
Capture HTTP response status and JSON body
expected: Response body contains cooling_off_ends_at timestamp approximately 24 hours from now
trace: R-FIN-086: beneficiary saved in pending state
tests.executable:
def test_add_beneficiary_returns_pending_state(auth_headers, api_client, unique_account):
    idempotency_key = <generate_uuid>("test_add_beneficiary_returns_pending_state")
    payload = {
        "account_number": unique_account,
        "account_name": "Alice Tester",
        "bank_code": "001"
    }
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    resp = api_client.post("/beneficiaries", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    assert "id" in body
    assert "cooling_off_ends_at" in body
    ends_at = <parse_iso8601>(body["cooling_off_ends_at"])
    now = <utcnow>()
    delta_hours = (ends_at - now).total_seconds() / 3600
    assert 23.9 <= delta_hours <= 24.1

tests.name: test_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original_response
preconditions: A beneficiary has already been created with a known Idempotency-Key
actions: POST /beneficiaries with payload P1 and Idempotency-Key K1 (first call)
Record first response status code and body
POST /beneficiaries with identical payload P1 and same Idempotency-Key K1 (second call)
expected: Second response body is identical to first response body
trace: R-FIN-086: duplicate submission with same Idempotency-Key must return original result
tests.executable:
def test_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original_response(auth_headers, api_client, unique_account):
    idempotency_key = <generate_uuid>("test_duplicate_idempotency_key")
    payload = {
        "account_number": unique_account,
        "account_name": "Bob Idempotent",
        "bank_code": "002"
    }
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    first = api_client.post("/beneficiaries", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert first.status_code == 201
    first_body = first.json()

    second = api_client.post("/beneficiaries", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert second.status_code == 201
    second_body = second.json()

    assert second_body["id"] == first_body["id"]
    assert second_body["status"] == first_body["status"]
    assert second_body["cooling_off_ends_at"] == first_body["cooling_off_ends_at"]

    beneficiaries = api_client.get("/beneficiaries", headers=auth_headers).json()
    matches = [b for b in beneficiaries if b["account_number"] == unique_account]
    assert len(matches) == 1

tests.name: test_duplicate_account_number_returns_409
preconditions: A beneficiary with account_number X already exists for this customer
actions: POST /beneficiaries with account_number X and a new Idempotency-Key (first call succeeds)
POST /beneficiaries with the same account_number X but a different Idempotency-Key (second call)
expected: Second response body contains error code DUPLICATE_ACCOUNT or equivalent
trace: R-FIN-086: error pathway — 409 if duplicate account

tests.name: test_beneficiary_status_transitions_to_active_after_cooling_off
preconditions: System clock can be manipulated via <advance_clock_fixture> or a test backdoor exists
Beneficiary has been created and is in pending state
actions: Advance system clock by exactly 24 hours using <advance_clock_fixture>
expected: Before clock advance: status == 'pending', transfers rejected
After exactly 24 hours: status transitions to 'active'
Transfer attempt after activation returns success (not cooling-off error)
trace: R-FIN-086: 24-hour cooling-off period before beneficiary can receive transfers
R-FIN-086: beneficiary saved in pending state (boundary at expiry)
tests.executable:
def test_beneficiary_status_transitions_to_active_after_cooling_off(
    auth_headers, api_client, unique_account, advance_clock_fixture
):
    payload = {
        "account_number": unique_account,
        "account_name": "Dave Boundary",
        "bank_code": "004"
    }
    key = <generate_uuid>("test_cooling_off_boundary")
    create_resp = api_client.post(
        "/beneficiaries",
        json=payload,
        headers={**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": key}
    )
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201
    beneficiary_id = create_resp.json()["id"]

    pending = api_client.get(f"/beneficiaries/{beneficiary_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert pending.json()["status"] == "pending"

    advance_clock_fixture(hours=24)

    active = api_client.get(f"/beneficiaries/{beneficiary_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert active.json()["status"] == "active"

tests.name: test_missing_idempotency_key_header_is_rejected
actions: POST /beneficiaries with valid payload but without Idempotency-Key header
expected: HTTP 400 Bad Request or 422 Unprocessable Entity
Response body contains error indicating Idempotency-Key is required
trace: R-FIN-086: idempotency key is a mandatory protocol contract for the operation
tests.executable:
def test_missing_idempotency_key_header_is_rejected(auth_headers, api_client, unique_account):
    payload = {
        "account_number": unique_account,
        "account_name": "Eve NoKey",
        "bank_code": "005"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/beneficiaries", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)

    assert resp.status_code in (400, 422)
    body = resp.json()
    raw = str(body).lower()
    assert "idempotency" in raw or "idempotency-key" in raw or "idempotency_key" in raw

tests.name: test_beneficiary_account_number_at_max_length_boundary
preconditions: Maximum allowed account number length is known (assumed 34 chars per IBAN spec)
actions: POST /beneficiaries with account_number of exactly 34 characters
POST /beneficiaries with account_number of exactly 35 characters (one over max)
expected: 34-character account number: HTTP 201, beneficiary created in pending state
35-character account number: HTTP 400 or 422, validation error on account_number field
trace: R-FIN-086: beneficiary saved in pending state (valid input boundary)
R-FIN-086: error pathway for invalid account data
tests.executable:
def test_beneficiary_account_number_at_max_length_boundary(auth_headers, api_client):
    max_len = 34
    valid_account = "A" * max_len
    over_account = "A" * (max_len + 1)

    valid_key = <generate_uuid>("test_bva_valid")
    valid_resp = api_client.post(
        "/beneficiaries",
        json={"account_number": valid_account, "account_name": "Frank BVA", "bank_code": "006"},
        headers={**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": valid_key}
    )
    assert valid_resp.status_code == 201
    assert valid_resp.json()["status"] == "pending"

    over_key = <generate_uuid>("test_bva_over")
    over_resp = api_client.post(
        "/beneficiaries",
        json={"account_number": over_account, "account_name": "Frank BVA Over", "bank_code": "006"},
        headers={**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": over_key}
    )
    assert over_resp.status_code in (400, 422)
    body = over_resp.json()
    assert "account_number" in str(body).lower()
